figures.caption: Figure 1. Sample quality vs. generation budget on QM9 dataset under filtering-based test-time scaling for EDM and GeoLDM diffusion models. We generate N ∈ {10K, 11K, . . . , 20K} molecules and keep the 10K with the lowest uncertainty or lowest diffusion NLL ; random subsampling and the no-filter setting at N = 10K serve as references. Uncertainty-based filtering improves molecular stability, atomic stability, and validity on both models and consistently outperforms NLL-based filtering, at the cost of a ∼1% drop in uniqueness. Notably, the EDM gains from test-time filtering are comparable in magnitude to those from switching the underlying model to GeoLDM at the 10K generation budget.

Figure 2. Same setup as Figure 1, evaluated on GEOM-Drugs. In contrast to QM9, neither uncertainty- nor NLL-based filtering meaningfully improves molecular stability, atom stability, or validity over random subsampling on this larger, more complex dataset.

Figure 3. Spearman rank correlation between single-timestep score uncertainty ut and atomic stability, as a function of timestep t. Signal concentrates sharply at the clean end of the generation trajectory (t ≈ 91) for both EDM and GeoLDM; measuring uncertainty at the start of the generation (large t) provides weaker signal.
